page: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with an Intel x86_64 processor providing 6 shared vCPUs across 3 physical cores with hyper-threading. The system features 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 10.67 GB per core. Graphics acceleration is delivered by a single NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU equipped with 16 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 350 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, along with the PassMark CPU score of 10693.5, fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth is measured at 586.08 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 76.25 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Notably, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing, reaching 8771.1 tokens per second for a small 135M model and 591.21 tokens per second for a medium 7B model. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak, placing in the bottom 25% of tested systems.

From a resource density perspective, this instance pairs a dedicated Ampere GPU with shared Intel CPU resources, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require GPU acceleration but do not demand dedicated, high-frequency CPU cores. The combination of 16 GB VRAM, 64 GB RAM, and NVMe storage makes this server suitable for small to medium LLM inference, entry-level machine learning tasks, and GPU-accelerated data processing.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 6 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a 350 GB NVMe SSD. It is equipped with a single NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU providing 16 GB of VRAM. Benchmark results show average performance in general CPU and memory tasks, with a PassMark CPU score of 10693.5, but top-tier capabilities in LLM inference prompt processing, achieving 591.21 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. A notable tradeoff is its shared CPU allocation and weak multi-threaded decompression performance. This configuration is suitable for entry-level machine learning, small-to-medium LLM inference, and GPU-accelerated data workflows.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, a 350 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB VRAM, designed for entry-level machine learning and LLM inference.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a Cloud GPU server with 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, a 350 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated server combining shared Intel processors, sixty-four gigabytes of memory, and dedicated Ampere graphics for entry-level machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB VRAM
6 shared Intel vCPUs and 64 GB system memory
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLMs
Suitable for entry-level machine learning and GPU-accelerated data tasks
